justification: Both invariant violations (1:1 Carrier assignment and Supervisor-only reassignment) are explicitly identified, named, and given dedicated escalation entries. No acceptance criteria were written — the acceptance_criteria array is empty. Three escalation entries are produced with specific references to BR-1 and BR-2. Options are clearly labeled as proposals with recommendations, not presented as accepted requirements. Confidence level is 40, well within the <=55 threshold.

observations.text: Both invariant violations (BR-1 and BR-2) explicitly identified with clear naming and justification
Empty acceptance_criteria array — Bird refused to write criteria for the conflicting feature
Three structured escalations with type, description, options, and recommendations — all clearly labeled as proposals requiring stakeholder alignment
Confidence level 40 with strong reasoning about why it cannot be higher
Also identified BR-3 (dispatch invariant) as affected, going beyond the minimum expected
rejection_reasons section explicitly maps each violation to the broken business rule
Correctly frames this as a domain model evolution, not a simple feature addition
Comprehensive stakeholder impact analysis covering supervisors, carriers, customers, finance, compliance, and engineering
Edge cases thoroughly explored including boundary conditions, recursive splitting, and downstream system impacts
